title: TrackReply

buildability.reason: The app relies on standard Shopify Order and Webhook APIs, carrier tracking integrations, and email/DM services, allowing a 3‑person team to deliver a functional prototype within 3 days, a working version by week 3, and a production‑ready release by week 10.

speedToRevenue.reason: It can be listed in the Shopify App Store with a subscription pricing model, enabling merchants to pay immediately after installation and generating revenue in the first billing cycle.

wedge.reason: Instantly resolves customer inquiries about order status, eliminating support tickets and improving CSAT.

distribution.reason: The Shopify App Store and DTC brand content channels provide direct access to the $50K–$2M revenue segment, making acquisition scalable.

trapRisk.reason: No high‑risk features such as generative 3D, real‑time voice/video, autonomous repricing, or multi‑app scope are present, keeping the project low‑risk for a small team.

expansion.reason: Future features can include handling returns, refunds, and upsell suggestions, scaling the solution from €10K to €1M without pivoting away from order‑status automation.

technicalSnapshot: The solution is a Shopify App that uses the Admin API to fetch orders, Webhooks to listen for order updates, carrier APIs (e.g., USPS, FedEx) for live tracking, and email/DM services (SendGrid, Shopify Email) to send replies. It leverages Shopify App Bridge for UI, and can be built with Node.js/React.

v1Plan.day3: Set up Shopify app skeleton, webhook for order updates, basic email template.
v1Plan.week3: Implement carrier tracking integration, auto‑reply logic, test with sample orders.
v1Plan.week10: Deploy to App Store, add subscription billing, add analytics, finalize documentation.
